exercises.title: if-else: Two-Way Decision
exercises.description: With if-else, exactly one branch always runs. Print the appropriate message for the given age.
exercises.starterCode:
age = 16

# TODO: If age >= 18 print 'You can vote!'
# else print how many years until they can vote
if age >= 18:
    print("You can vote!")
else:
    print(f"You can vote in {18 - age} years.")
hints: The else branch runs when the if condition is False.

exercises.title: elif Chain: Letter Grade
exercises.description: Use an elif chain to assign a letter grade. Only one branch should run for each score.
hints: The elif chain checks conditions in order and stops at the first True one.
score=83 is >= 80 so it gets a B (not checked against >= 70 after that).

exercises.title: Classifying Multiple Cases
exercises.description: Use if-elif-else to classify a temperature reading into categories and print the category and a recommendation.
exercises.starterCode:
temperature = 28  # Celsius

# Temperature categories:
# < 0       : Freezing
# 0-9       : Cold
# 10-19     : Cool
# 20-29     : Comfortable
# 30-39     : Warm
# >= 40     : Hot

if temperature < 0:
    category = "Freezing"
    advice = "Stay indoors!"
elif temperature < 10:
    category = "Cold"
    advice = "Wear a heavy coat."
elif temperature < 20:
    category = "Cool"
    advice = "Bring a jacket."
elif temperature < 30:
    category = "Comfortable"
    advice = "Enjoy the weather!"
elif temperature < 40:
    category = "Warm"
    advice = "Stay hydrated."
else:
    category = "Hot"
    advice = "Avoid going out."

print(f"{temperature}°C: {category}")
print(f"Advice: {advice}")
hints: 28 is >= 20 and < 30, so it falls in the 'Comfortable' category.
The elif chain checks from the lowest bound up.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: elif vs Multiple ifs
warmup.description: elif chains stop at the first True condition. Multiple ifs each check independently.
